privileged_info_summary: All ground truth metadata claims have been independently verified against the NOAA CAMEO Chemicals database. The task requires agents to research 5 chemicals (Acetone, Acetic Acid Glacial, Thionyl Chloride, Hydrochloric Acid, Ethylene Glycol) and determine their RCRA hazardous waste codes based on flash point, corrosivity, and water reactivity. Verified facts: Acetone (flash point 0°F, not corrosive, not water reactive → D001); Acetic Acid (104°F, corrosive, not water reactive → D001, D002); Thionyl Chloride (no flash point data, corrosive, violently reacts with water → D002, D003); Hydrochloric Acid (no flash point data, corrosive, not water reactive → D002); Ethylene Glycol (232°F, not corrosive, not water reactive → no codes). All RCRA code determinations in the ground truth are correct according to applicable regulations.
